# v1.0.0 Performance Baseline

Pre-M7 capture per L-61 evaluator Finding #9. All `SV-PERF-*` test IDs (landing at M11 Gateway load, M12 observability load, M13 admin-UI load) diff against the numbers pinned here.

Captured 2026-04-23 at v1.0.0. Impl source: `@soa-harness/*@1.0.0` installed from the public npm registry via `pnpm install --prod`. This is more honest than benching against a local impl checkout because it measures the artifact adopters actually receive.

**Do not edit the numbers below** once this doc lands — subsequent releases produce a sibling `docs/m7/perf-v1.Y.md` and the delta table lives in the release notes. This file is the frozen anchor.

## What "baseline" means here

- These are **Reference Implementation** numbers on consumer/server hardware. They are NOT a performance spec. A conforming impl is not required to match them.
- They are the **regression line**: if v1.1.0 or later makes any listed metric >2× worse on the same environment, release-gate surfaces a WARN; >3× surfaces a FAIL.
- They are **measured, not aspirational**. Every number in the tables below has a reproducible command and was run on the specific hardware documented in §Environments.
- **Single-client measurement.** `ops_per_sec_approx` is `1000 / p50_ms` under sequential load — honest for latency-per-request, not a concurrency benchmark. Real throughput under N parallel clients is an M11 benchmark (SV-PERF-01..04), not a v1.0 anchor.

## Metrics captured in v1.0 baseline

| # | Metric | Captured? | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Cold boot to `/ready=200` | ✅ | in-process (no real CRL fetch — fixtures only) |
| 2 | Agent Card sign | ✅ | in-process (pure function) |
| 3 | Agent Card endpoint | ✅ | BOTH in-process (`app.inject`) and over HTTP (loopback fetch) |
| 4 | CRL cache miss → fetch → validate | ⏸ deferred | Requires real CRL endpoint harness — captured during M10 session-replay infra |
| 5 | JCS canonicalize (StreamEvent-shaped) | ✅ | isolates canonicalization cost from emitter + transport |
| 6 | Permission resolve (3-axis) | ⏸ deferred | Requires wired registry+session context — captured during M11 Gateway perf |
| 7 | Session persist fsync | ⏸ deferred | Requires M2 crash-test infra (not yet in v1.0 scope) |
| 8 | Audit append fsync | ⏸ deferred | Same — M2 crash-test infra |
| 9 | Runner RSS at idle | ✅ | post-GC settle, /ready=200 |
| 10 | Runner RSS under 100 req/s | ⏸ deferred | M12 observability milestone adds load harness |
| 11 | SHA-256 of 2 KiB | ✅ | crypto floor — proxy for per-byte hash throughput |

Four metrics are explicitly deferred. Each comes with a named later-milestone owner; none are orphaned. Metrics 4, 6, 7, 8, 10 are **internal-path** or **concurrency-under-load** measurements that the current bench harness can't capture honestly without more infra. Skipping them is better than faking them.

## Methodology

### Bench harness

`scripts/m7/bench-v1.0-baseline.mjs` in this repo. Two modes:
- `--impl-root <path>`: resolves `@soa-harness/*` from a local impl checkout's `packages/*/dist/`
- `--npm-mode --fixtures-dir <path>`: resolves from a `pnpm install`ed scratch dir — **used for the numbers below**

### Measurement protocol

- **Warmup**: 3 runs discarded (JIT warmup, Fastify route compile, JCS table build, jose key-cache prime)
- **Measured**: 10 runs
- **p50**: median of per-run medians
- **p95**: median of per-run 95th-percentiles (robust p95 — a single outlier run doesn't skew the anchor)
- **Sample sizes per run**: 1000 card signs, 1000 card-endpoint hits, 10000 JCS canonicalizations, 10000 SHA-256s
- **Cold boot**: 10 separate process starts; median + min + max reported

### Two surface levels

Agent Card endpoint is captured at both surfaces:
- **In-process** (`app.inject()`): pure Runner overhead, no network stack. Isolates crypto/JCS/cache hit from Fastify HTTP parse+serialize. SV-PERF-* gates in M11+ diff against HTTP numbers; in-process numbers exist to localize future regressions (crypto layer vs HTTP layer).
- **Over HTTP** (`fetch` → listener on `127.0.0.1`): realistic adopter-facing latency; includes socket + Fastify overhead. **This is the anchor SV-PERF-* diffs against.**

### Statistical honesty

Three-environment capture: each env is pinned separately; M11+ SV-PERF-* tests diff against the **same environment** they run on. Numbers are not averaged across environments.

Single-maintainer measurement on consumer hardware with unknown background-process noise. Don't tighten gate thresholds below 2× without a controlled CI environment.

## Environments

### Env A — Windows 11 native

| Attribute | Value |
|---|---|
| OS | Windows 11 Pro (10.0.26200) |
| CPU | 13th Gen Intel Core i9-13900K (32 logical CPUs) |
| RAM | 63.7 GB |
| Node | v22.19.0 |
| Filesystem | NTFS |
| Notes | Developer workstation. Represents the high-end adopter dev loop. |

### Env B — WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04 on native ext4

| Attribute | Value |
|---|---|
| OS | Ubuntu 24.04 under WSL2 kernel 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard |
| CPU | 13th Gen Intel Core i9-13900K (32 logical CPUs — shared with host) |
| RAM | 31.2 GB (WSL2 default split) |
| Node | v22.22.1 |
| Filesystem | ext4 on VHDX (native Linux path; NOT `/mnt/c`) |
| Notes | Represents "adopter did it right" — ext4 home dir under WSL2. A `/mnt/c`-hosted run was measured during bench development and showed materially slower fsync — adopters should avoid running Runner out of `/mnt/c/...`. |

### Env D — Ubuntu 24.04 on dedicated LAN hardware

| Attribute | Value |
|---|---|
| OS | Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS (kernel 6.8.0-100-generic) |
| CPU | Intel Xeon D-1541 @ 2.10 GHz (8 logical CPUs) |
| RAM | 5.8 GB |
| Node | v22.22.2 |
| Filesystem | ext4 bare metal |
| Host | 192.168.11.193 — proxy server previously used for v1.0.0 fresh-install smoke test |
| Notes | Low-power 2015-era Xeon D; represents a realistic edge / home-lab deployment floor. A modern Xeon Gold or Graviton will produce materially faster numbers. |

Env C (WSL2 on `/mnt/c`) is intentionally omitted — the slowdown is real but Env B already represents the "correct" WSL2 path and Env D dominates for production-shaped Linux. The raw `/mnt/c` data (`docs/m7/v1.0-perf-raw/env-b-wsl2-mntc.json`) is kept for reference but not promoted to the pinned table.

## Results

### Metric 1 — Cold boot to `/ready=200`

| Env | median ms | min | max |
|---|---|---|---|
| A — Windows 11 / i9-13900K | 2.63 | 2.32 | 5.44 |
| B — WSL2 Ubuntu / i9-13900K | 2.75 | 2.44 | 4.00 |
| D — Ubuntu bare-metal / Xeon D-1541 | 7.24 | 6.85 | 10.29 |

Cold-boot number reflects in-process boot with fixture trust anchors — **no real CRL fetch**. Production boot with network CRL validation will be materially slower (§7.3.1 CRL fetch ≥ one RTT + DNS). M11 will add a realistic cold-boot number once Gateway lands.

### Metric 2 — Agent Card sign (pure function, in-process)

| Env | p50 ms | p95 ms | min | max | ops/s (single-client) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A — Windows 11 / i9-13900K | 0.070 | 0.096 | 0.063 | 0.392 | ~14,300 |
| B — WSL2 Ubuntu / i9-13900K | 0.086 | 0.123 | 0.080 | 0.573 | ~11,600 |
| D — Ubuntu bare-metal / Xeon D-1541 | 0.258 | 0.552 | 0.182 | 2.057 | ~3,900 |

### Metric 3a — Agent Card endpoint (in-process `app.inject`)

ETag cache hits — Runner does not re-sign on every request; JWS is cached by canonical body hash.

| Env | p50 ms | p95 ms | min | max | ops/s |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A — Windows 11 / i9-13900K | 0.017 | 0.021 | 0.016 | 2.412 | ~58,800 |
| B — WSL2 Ubuntu / i9-13900K | 0.017 | 0.036 | 0.014 | 3.346 | ~58,800 |
| D — Ubuntu bare-metal / Xeon D-1541 | 0.066 | 0.100 | 0.050 | 6.361 | ~15,200 |

### Metric 3b — Agent Card endpoint (over HTTP, 127.0.0.1)

**This is the row SV-PERF-* diffs against.**

| Env | p50 ms | p95 ms | min | max | ops/s |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A — Windows 11 / i9-13900K | 0.135 | 0.202 | 0.118 | 2.867 | ~7,400 |
| B — WSL2 Ubuntu / i9-13900K | 0.075 | 0.123 | 0.064 | 2.548 | ~13,300 |
| D — Ubuntu bare-metal / Xeon D-1541 | 0.371 | 0.448 | 0.322 | 10.959 | ~2,700 |

Windows' HTTP numbers trail Linux's on the same hardware — the Windows TCP stack adds measurable loopback overhead. Not a defect; a characteristic to remember when interpreting adopter reports.

### Metric 5 — JCS canonicalize (StreamEvent-shaped object, ~200 bytes)

| Env | p50 ms | p95 ms | min | max | ops/s |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| A — Windows 11 / i9-13900K | 0.005 | 0.005 | 0.004 | 0.340 | ~200,000 |
| B — WSL2 Ubuntu / i9-13900K | 0.004 | 0.005 | 0.003 | 0.361 | ~250,000 |
| D — Ubuntu bare-metal / Xeon D-1541 | 0.008 | 0.010 | 0.007 | 0.750 | ~125,000 |

### Metric 9 — Runner RSS at idle

| Env | MB |
|---|---|
| A — Windows 11 / i9-13900K | 219.7 |
| B — WSL2 Ubuntu / i9-13900K | 283.0 |
| D — Ubuntu bare-metal / Xeon D-1541 | 237.1 |

WSL2's higher idle is consistent with its longer-lived worker process patterns — not a runner-specific leak. These numbers are sanity anchors; M12 observability milestone will add a steady-state-under-load growth canary.

### Metric 11 — SHA-256 of 2 KiB buffer

| Env | p50 ms | p95 ms | ops/s |
|---|---|---|---|
| A — Windows 11 / i9-13900K | 0.002 | 0.002 | ~500,000 |
| B — WSL2 Ubuntu / i9-13900K | 0.001 | 0.001 | ~1,000,000 |
| D — Ubuntu bare-metal / Xeon D-1541 | 0.007 | 0.008 | ~142,900 |

## How future SV-PERF-* tests use this

| Test ID | Milestone | Metric(s) gated | Gate threshold |
|---|---|---|---|
| SV-PERF-01 | M11 | Metric 1 (cold boot) + 3b (card HTTP) | Within 2× of baseline for same env row |
| SV-PERF-02 | M11 | Metric 6 (permission resolve, new capture) | Within 2× — capture happens at M11, pins into v1.4 baseline |
| SV-PERF-03 | M11 | Metric 10 (RSS under load, new capture) | Peak within 1.5× baseline |
| SV-PERF-04 | M11 | Gateway-fronted Metric 3b | Absolute threshold (Gateway adds overhead; no prior baseline) |
| SV-PERF-05..08 | M12 | Observability-instrumented 2, 3b, 5, 11 | Within 1.25× (observability adds small overhead) |
| SV-PERF-09..12 | M13 | Admin-UI concurrent ops | Absolute threshold — new capability |

Gate thresholds deliberately loose because the baseline was captured by a single maintainer on consumer hardware. Tightening happens once M11+ CI runs in a controlled environment with pinned reference hardware.
